mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 4% show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05) across 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and federal funds rates signal central bank tightening that raises equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 4% show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly acceleration in GDP growth above 3% signals robust economic demand that supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3% q/q show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weak demand for construction, reducing corporate revenue visibility and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with HOUST < 1400 show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment rates signals a strong labor market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 4.5% show negative or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below 730 billion dollars signals weak consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with RSAFS < 730 show positive or neutral performance (Rank IC < 0.05).
